message:
feat(daemon): add swap-thrash detection and temp artifact fast-track deletion under pressure

Two reliability improvements to the storage ballast monitoring daemon:

1. Swap-thrash risk detection: monitor swap usage relative to available
   RAM and emit SBH-2010 error events when swap usage exceeds 70% while
   sufficient RAM remains free (indicating kernel paging pathology rather
   than genuine memory exhaustion). Warnings are rate-limited with a
   15-minute cooldown to avoid log spam during sustained pressure.

2. Temp artifact age fast-tracking: under Orange/Red pressure levels,
   recognized temp artifacts in /tmp-like paths have their candidacy age
   adjusted to meet min_file_age thresholds, allowing the scanner to
   reclaim well-known ephemeral build artifacts more aggressively while
   still respecting a 2-minute minimum observed age to protect actively
   written files.

Also refactors the executor policy-engine lock scope to minimize hold
duration (pure computation was already fast, but the eprintln! call
no longer holds the mutex).

Includes unit tests for:
- fast-track age adjustment under Red pressure
- fast-track skip for non-tmp paths and low pressure levels
- minimum observed age protection for very fresh artifacts
- swap-thrash risk detection with RAM/swap threshold combinations

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
